utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi , andrew . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hey , good to see you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm doing well , i'm doing well .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , i know the nurse told you about dax . i'd like to tell dax a little bit about you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: uh , so , andrew is a 59-year-old male with a past medical history , significant for depression , type two diabetes , and hypertension who presents today with an upper respiratory infection . so , andrew , what's going on ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . we were doing a bit of work out in the yard in the last week or so and i started to feel really tired , was short of breath . um , we- we're not wearing masks as much at the end of the summer and i think i caught my first cold and i think it just got worse .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . um , now , have you had your covid vaccines ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , both .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and , um , do you have any history of any seasonal allergies at all ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: none whatsoever .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and when you say you're having some shortness of breath , did you feel short of breath walking around or at rest ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , usually , it was lifting or carrying something . we were doing some landscaping , so i was carrying some heavy bags of soil and i , i got really winded . it really surprised me .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and are you coughing up anything ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not yet , but i feel like that's next .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and fevers ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , i felt a little warm , but i , i just thought it was because i was exerting myself .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and any other symptoms like muscle aches , joint pain , fatigue ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: my elbows hurt quite a bit and my knees were pretty tired . l- like i said , i really felt some tension around my knees , but , uh , i think that was a lot to do with , uh , lifting the bags .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . um , so , you know , how about , how are you doing in terms of your other medical problems , like your depression ? how are you doing with that ? i know we've , you know , talked about not putting you on medication for it because you're on medication for other things . what's going on ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i- it's been kind of a crazy year and a half . i was a little concerned about that but , for the most part , i've been , been doing well with it . my , my wife got me into barre classes , to help me relax and i think it's working .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right , great . and , and in terms of your diabetes , how are you doing watching your , your diet and your sugar intake ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , i've been monitoring my sugar levels while i am going to work during the week . uh , not so , uh , if its saturday or sunday i usually don't remember . uh , the diet's been pretty good for the most part , except for , you know , some house parties and things like that . but , uh , been good for the most part .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and have they been elevated at all since this episode of your-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and then , how , lastly , for your high blood pressure , have you been monitoring your blood pressures at home ? did you buy the cuff like i suggested ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , same thing . during the while i'm going to work, i'm regular about monitoring it, but if its a saturday or sunday, not so much . but , uh , it's , it's been under control .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: but you're taking your medication ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . well , you know , i know that , you know , you've endorsed , you know , the shortness of breath and some joint pain . um , how about any other symptoms ? nausea or vomiting ? diarrhea ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: anything like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . well , i wan na go ahead and do a quick physical exam , all right ? hey , dragon , show me the vital signs . so , your vital signs here in the office look quite good .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you know , everything's looking normal , you do n't have a fever , which is really good . um , i'm just gon na go ahead and listen to your heart and your lungs and , kind of , i'll let you know what i hear , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so , on your physical exam , you know , your heart sounds nice and strong . your lungs , you do have scattered ronchi bilaterally on your lung exam . uh , it clears with cough . um , i do notice a little bit of , um , some edema of your lower extremities and you do have some pain to palpation of your elbows bilaterally . um , so , let's go ahead , i want to look at some of your results , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon . show me the chest x-ray .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , i reviewed the results of your chest x-ray and everything looks good . there's no airspace disease , there's no pneumonia , so that's all very , very good , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon . show me the diabetic labs .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and here , looking at your diabetic labs , you know , your hemoglobin a1c is a little elevated at eight .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'd like to see that a little bit better , around six or seven , if possible .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , so let's talk a little bit about my assessment and my plan for you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , for your first problem , this upper respiratory infection , i believe you , you have a viral syndrome , okay ? we'll go ahead and we'll send a covid test , just to make sure that you do n't have covid .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: uh , but overall , i think that , um , you know , this will resolve in a couple of days . i do n't think you have covid , you do n't have any exposures , that type of thing .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , i think that this will improve . i'll give you some robitussin for your cough and i would encourage you take some ibuprofen , tylenol for any fever , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you got it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: for your next problem , your depression , you know , it sounds like you're doing well with that , but again , i'm happy to start on a med- , a medical regiment or ...

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . refer you to psychotherapy , if you think that that would be helpful .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: would you like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: u- u- um , maybe not necessarily . maybe in a , uh , few months we'll check on that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: for your third problem , your type two diabetes , i want to go ahead and increase your metformin to 1000 milligrams , twice daily .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and i'm gon na get an- another hemoglobin a1c in four months , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon . order a hemoglobin a1c .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and lastly , for your high blood pressure , it looks like you're doing a really good job managing that . i want to go ahead and continue you on the , um , lisinopril , 20 milligrams a day .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and i'm gon na go ahead and order a lipid panel , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: do you need a refill of the lisinopril ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: actually , i do .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . hey , dragon . order lisinopril , 20 milligrams daily .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , the nurse will be in , she'll help you , uh , make a follow-up appointment with me . i want to see you again in about four months .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: let me know if your symptoms worsen and we can talk more about it , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you got it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . hey , dragon . finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 59-year-old male with a history of depression, type 2 diabetes, and hypertension presenting with an upper respiratory infection.
The patient reports fatigue, shortness of breath, and symptoms worsening after yard work and lack of mask-wearing.
The doctor asks if the patient has received COVID-19 vaccinations.
The patient confirms he has received both COVID-19 vaccinations.
The doctor asks if the patient has a history of seasonal allergies.
The patient denies any history of seasonal allergies.
The doctor asks if the shortness of breath occurs at rest or with exertion.
The patient reports shortness of breath occurs during physical exertion like lifting heavy bags.
The doctor asks if the patient is coughing up any phlegm.
The patient denies currently coughing up phlegm but feels it may start soon.
The doctor asks if the patient has a fever.
The patient denies a fever but felt warm due to physical exertion.
The doctor asks about other symptoms including muscle aches, joint pain, and fatigue.
The patient reports elbow pain and knee tension, attributing them to lifting heavy bags.
Depression
The doctor asks how the patient is managing his depression.
The patient reports doing well with depression and finds barre classes relaxing.
Diabetes
The doctor asks how the patient is managing his diet and blood sugar.
The patient monitors blood sugar during the work week but is inconsistent on weekends; diet is generally good despite occasional house parties.
The doctor asks if blood sugar levels have been elevated since the respiratory symptoms began.
The patient denies elevated blood sugar levels since the onset of respiratory symptoms.
Hypertension
The doctor asks if the patient is monitoring his blood pressure at home.
The patient monitors blood pressure regularly during the work week but less so on weekends, and reports it is under control.
The doctor asks if the patient is taking his blood pressure medication.
The patient confirms he is taking his blood pressure medication.
The doctor asks about nausea, vomiting, or diarrhea.
The patient denies nausea, vomiting, or diarrhea.
The doctor notes that the patient's vital signs are normal and he is afebrile.
Physical exam findings include scattered rhonchi that clear with cough, lower extremity edema, and pain to palpation of the elbows.
The chest x-ray is unremarkable with no signs of pneumonia or airspace disease.
The patient's hemoglobin A1c is elevated at 8.
The doctor assesses the patient with a viral syndrome and will order a COVID-19 test.
The doctor recommends Robitussin for cough and ibuprofen or Tylenol for fever.
The doctor offers medication or psychotherapy for depression.
The patient declines medication or psychotherapy for depression at this time.
The doctor increases metformin to 1000 mg twice daily.
The doctor will repeat the hemoglobin A1c in four months.
The doctor continues lisinopril 20 mg daily.
The doctor will order a lipid panel.
The doctor asks if the patient needs a refill of lisinopril.
The patient confirms he needs a refill of lisinopril.
The doctor orders a refill of lisinopril 20 mg daily.
The doctor schedules a follow-up appointment in four months.

case_description: upper respiratory infection; depression;type two diabetes;hypertension
